output:
CVE-2020-36162 is a high-severity vulnerability in Veritas CloudPoint versions before 8.3.0.1+hotfix. The flaw exists because the CloudPoint Windows Agent uses an OpenSSL library that attempts to load a non-existent configuration file located at \\usr\\local\\ssl\\openssl.cnf. On Windows systems, a low privileged user can exploit this by creating a directory and configuration file at that path, allowing the loading of a malicious OpenSSL engine. This can lead to remote code execution (RCE), granting the attacker administrative privileges on the targeted system.

The vulnerability is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1574, which involves the abuse of external configuration and data storage. Although the exact CWE is unlisted (NVD-CWE-noinfo), the described attack pattern typically relates to improper configuration vulnerabilities.

Affected versions of the software include various early releases of Veritas CloudPoint and NetBackup CloudPoint. Mitigation techniques include restricting the ability to create specific directories, enhancing monitoring and access controls, and applying the latest patches provided by Veritas. These security measures can prevent unauthorized configuration changes and mitigate the risk of exploitation.
